title: GitHub - michaelolmos/tsaagan: Human-like browser control for AI agents — verify-first (proves every action worked), with an MCP server + JS & Python SDKs. Playwright + native + extension. MIT.
excerpt: Human-like browser control for AI agents — verify-first (proves every action worked), with an MCP server + JS & Python SDKs. Playwright + native + extension. MIT. - michaelolmos/tsaagan
content_html:
Tsaagan gives your AI agent real hands on the web — so it can finish the browser work you'd otherwise stop and do yourself (log in, click through, fill forms, pull data from behind a login), on the tasks you're authorized to do. (Full mission below.)

Human-like browser control for AI agents. Tsaagan lets an AI drive a real browser the way a person does — perceive the page, click, type, navigate, handle logins, popups, iframes, downloads — and, crucially, verify that each action actually worked before moving on.

The mental model: your agent is the brain, Tsaagan is the hands. Whatever AI drives it (Claude, a Codex/ChatGPT CLI, Cursor, or any model) supplies intent and judgment; Tsaagan supplies reliable perception → action → verification → memory. The agent decides what; Tsaagan reliably does how, and proves it worked.

Mission

Tsaagan gives your AI agent real hands on the web. It lets an agent autonomously do the browser work that today still makes you stop and take over — logging in, clicking through, filling forms, pulling data from behind a login — and gives that time back to you.

One-liner: Tsaagan is the part of your agentic workflow that actually drives the browser — reliably, like a human, on the tasks you're authorized to do.

The mental model — Tsaagan is the hands; your agent is the brain

You can think with an agent, write with an agent, code with an agent — but the moment the work needs a browser ("log in here, click through there, grab this, submit that"), the agent stalls and you become the hands. Tsaagan closes that gap. The brain (Claude, Codex, a small open model, or a person) supplies intent and judgment; Tsaagan supplies reliable perception → action → verification → memory.

Three ideas make it click:

  1. It hovers, then strikes. Like the bird — patient perception, then one exact move. It reads the page's accessibility tree, picks the right element, acts, and then proves the action worked from real page state (URL changed? expected text? errors?) instead of clicking and hoping. That verify step is the difference between "automation that mostly works" and automation you can trust unattended.
  2. Lightest tool that does the job. If a site has an API, use the API. If not, drive the real browser like a human (trusted input). Only fall back to raw automation for the broad middle of the web.
  3. It remembers and improves. It keeps an inspectable, file-based memory of the sites it works — learned selectors, what went wrong last time — so it gets better at your recurring tasks.

What it does for the user

  • Turns "every week I log into X, click through, and copy Y" into "my agent does it and reports back what it verified."
  • Works on real, logged-in sites — dashboards, portals, tools with no API — where a scraper or WebFetch can't go.
  • Keeps you in control: it stops and hands back at CAPTCHAs, and flags consequential, irreversible actions (buy, send, delete). Empowerment, not autopilot-into-a-wall.

What Tsaagan deliberately is not

Tsaagan is not a tool for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, or doing anything against a site's Terms of Service. It is for builders and creators who want their agents to finish the job on work they're already authorized to do — and it's honest about its limits rather than overselling. Only automate accounts and systems you own or are authorized to access. When it meets a CAPTCHA or an anti-abuse wall, it stops and hands control back to you. See the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer — you are solely responsible for how you use it.

Philosophy — why I built Tsaagan

I kept hitting the same wall in my own agentic workflows: you can reason, write, and code with an agent — but the moment a task needs a browser (log in, click through, pull this, file that), the agent stalls and a human has to take over. That handoff is where the time goes.

Tsaagan exists to give your AI agents real hands — so they can autonomously do that web work and give the time back to you. It's for builders and creators who want their agents to actually finish the job, not just plan it.

And it's built on a clear ethic: empowerment, not abuse. Tsaagan deliberately does not defeat CAPTCHAs, asks for human confirmation on consequential actions, stores secrets only in your OS keychain, keeps everything local, and respects site Terms of Service. It exists to extend what you can build — not to create risk. That ethic is encoded in SOUL.md and enforced in AGENTS.md.

Use it on tasks you're authorized to do. Tsaagan is for automating your own accounts and authorized work — not for unauthorized access or circumventing security. Please read the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer.

— Michael Olmos, creator

Capability layers — use the lightest one that does the job

  1. API layer — if a site has an official API, Tsaagan calls it directly with a key kept in your Keychain (fastest and most reliable). The browser layers can even set the key up for you. → docs/API.md
  2. Real-browser layer — extension (any OS) / native (macOS): drives your real Chrome with trusted input, no debug port — for sites that ignore synthetic input. → docs/EXTENSION.md
  3. CDP layer — Playwright on the real Chrome binary for the broad middle of the web.

Plus an agent layer (identity + brain memory) that makes Tsaagan a self-improving agent, not just a tool. → docs/AGENT.md

Memory that learns (a real loop, not just storage)

Tsaagan doesn't just remember — it learns from each run and feeds the lesson back:

  • Reflect: a successful run is stored as a reusable procedure; a failure becomes a one-line lesson (via an LLM reflection); an anti-abuse wall flips the domain to pace: human. Accumulated lessons are synthesized into durable rules.
  • Feed back: every snapshot carries a memory block (rules, lessons, pace, success track) — so the driving agent (Claude, Codex, or any LLM) adapts before it acts.
  • Research-grade recall: ranked by relevance (embeddings) × importance × recency, not substring match. Zero-dependency by default; real neural embeddings if TSG_EMBED_URL or @xenova/transformers is present.
  • Look-ahead + evaluator gate: it imagines the outcome before irreversible actions, and only stores a procedure when success is validator- and structurally-verified.

Memory lives on your machine (~/.tsaagan/brain.db) — not in a model, not in the cloud. → docs/AGENT.md

Most browser-agent stacks fail for the same two reasons: they ground actions on ephemeral element ids that break the moment the page changes, and they trust the model's guess that an action succeeded. Tsaagan fixes both:

  • Stable, self-healing grounding. Elements are addressed by accessibility-tree refs; if a ref goes stale, Tsaagan re-snapshots and re-locates the same element by role + name automatically.
  • Structural verification. After every action Tsaagan returns a verify block built from real page state — URL delta, expected text, new console errors, failed network requests — so the agent knows whether it worked.

It's three things in one:

  1. A CLI + daemon you (or a coding agent) drive verb-by-verb.
  2. A library (agent.js) with an autonomous planner→navigator→validator loop.
  3. A standalone server you hand goals to over HTTP — no agent framework required.

Why Tsaagan

How Tsaagan compares

The browser-agent space is crowded — and Tsaagan is the new, small project here. It doesn't try to out-scale browser-use or out-distribute Microsoft's playwright-mcp. It wins on a different axis: reliability you can audit. The one capability every other tool lacks — structural proof that each action worked — is Tsaagan's core.

Honest notes. Tsaagan is young — fewer stars and no hosted option, and its benchmark number is still being run (published scores across tools use different WebVoyager task subsets and aren't directly comparable). What it uniquely offers: proof-of-success on every action, an API-first fast path, a local credential vault, and trusted-input modes that need no debug port.

How it works — tool, skill, or agent?

Tsaagan is one engine you can drive several ways:

  • The tool — a CLI (tsaagan.js) talking to a background daemon that holds a single persistent Playwright page. This is the engine; everything else wraps it.
  • As a skill — a short instruction file that teaches your AI how to drive the tool (the observe → act → verify loop). "Use the Tsaagan skill" → the AI reads it and drives Tsaagan step by step. (A ready-made skill for Claude Code is included.)
  • As an agent — a dispatchable worker that bundles the skill + the tool, so you can hand it a goal and it runs the loop on its own.
  • As an MCP server or SDK — tsaagan mcp exposes the verify-first verbs to Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor, and the JS + Python SDKs call the same daemon directly. (See Use it from Claude/Cursor (MCP) and Use it from your own code above.)

Who is the "brain"? Whatever drives it:

You drive it with… Brain API key needed
Claude Code (the skill/agent) your Claude session none — your Claude plan
Codex / ChatGPT CLI, Cursor, etc. that tool's model that tool's
tsaagan run / tsaagan serve (autonomous) any OpenAI-compatible LLM — Groq · OpenRouter · OpenAI · Google Gemini · Anthropic · xAI Grok · local a key for your chosen provider

Tsaagan itself makes no LLM calls when an agent drives it — it's pure perception + action + verification. Only the autonomous tiers call an LLM, and that LLM is your choice: Groq (default, fast), OpenRouter for open-source models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral), the enterprise vendors — OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic, xAI Grok (each via its OpenAI-compatible endpoint) — or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Together, vLLM, Ollama for fully local) via TSG_LLM_BASE_URL. Pick one with TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai. See docs/AGENT.md.

Use it with any coding agent

Tsaagan is just a terminal program, so any agent that can run shell commands can drive it. Tell your agent something like:

"Use Tsaagan (node tsaagan.js) to log into example.com and download my invoices. Snapshot, act on refs, and check the verify block after each step."

It will run tsaagan start, tsaagan snapshot, tsaagan click ref=…, read the JSON results, and proceed. For Claude Code, the bundled skill/agent make this automatic.

Install

git clone https://github.com/michaelolmos/tsaagan.git && cd tsaagan
npm install
npx playwright install chromium chromium-headless-shell

Quick start (drive it yourself)

node tsaagan.js start                    # launch browser (fresh isolated profile)
node tsaagan.js goto url=https://example.com expectText="Example"
node tsaagan.js snapshot                 # accessibility tree with [ref=eN]
node tsaagan.js click ref=e6 expectText="IANA"
node tsaagan.js type ref=e3 text="hello" submit=true
node tsaagan.js stop

Add an alias: alias tsaagan="node $PWD/tsaagan.js".

Use it from Claude Desktop, Claude Code, or Cursor (MCP)

Tsaagan ships a built-in MCP server, so any Model Context Protocol host can drive the browser directly. Unlike other browser MCP servers, every mutating tool returns Tsaagan's verify block (URL changed? console errors? expected text?) — proof the action worked, in the same response, with no extra snapshot round-trip.

Claude Code:

claude mcp add tsaagan -- node /path/to/tsaagan/tsaagan.js mcp
# or, if you've installed the `tsaagan` bin globally:  claude mcp add tsaagan -- tsaagan mcp

Claude Desktop (claude_desktop_config.json) or Cursor (~/.cursor/mcp.json):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "tsaagan": { "command": "node", "args": ["/path/to/tsaagan/tsaagan.js", "mcp"] }
  }
}

The server auto-starts a headless daemon on first use. It exposes 21 verify-first tools — tsaagan_navigate, tsaagan_snapshot, tsaagan_click, tsaagan_fill_form, tsaagan_extract, tsaagan_network (discover a site's own data API), and more. Set TSAAGAN_HEADLESS=0 to watch it work, or TSG_TOKEN to lock the control plane on shared machines. See docs/PROTOCOL.md.

Use it from your own code (JS & Python SDK)

Prefer calling Tsaagan programmatically? Both clients return a verify block on every call — data and proof, together (the thing other tools make you assert yourself).

JavaScript / TypeScript (sdk/, zero dependencies):

import { createTsaagan } from 'tsaagan/sdk';   // or '/path/to/tsaagan/sdk/index.mjs'

const k = await createTsaagan();                // auto-starts a headless daemon
await k.goto('https://example.com', { expectText: 'Example Domain' });
const r = await k.extract('the page heading');
console.log(r.data, r.verify);                  // data + { urlChanged, expectTextFound, newConsoleErrors, ... }
await k.stop();

Python (pip install tsaagan, zero dependencies):

from tsaagan import Tsaagan

k = Tsaagan()                                   # auto-starts a headless daemon
k.goto("https://example.com", expect_text="Example Domain")
r = k.extract("the page heading")
print(r.data, r.verify)
k.stop()

The Python client needs the Node daemon reachable — set TSAAGAN_JS=/path/to/tsaagan.js or put the tsaagan binary on PATH. See clients/python/.

Autonomous mode

export GROQ_API_KEY=...                   # the autonomous brain (fast default)

# …or any other provider — name it and supply that provider's key:
export OPENROUTER_API_KEY=...   TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter   # open-source models
export OPENAI_API_KEY=...       TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=openai       # GPT-4o / o-series
export GEMINI_API_KEY=...       TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=google       # Gemini
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...    TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic    # Claude
export XAI_API_KEY=...          TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=xai          # Grok
export TSG_LLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions  # Ollama, fully local

node tsaagan.js run goal="go to news.ycombinator.com and report the #1 story title"
node tsaagan.js journal                   # what it has done before (persistent memory)

Per-role model overrides work with any provider: TSG_PLANNER_MODEL, TSG_NAV_MODEL, TSG_VALIDATOR_MODEL (e.g. point the planner at a stronger model for reasoning while a fast model handles navigation).

Standalone server (no agent framework)

node tsaagan.js serve port=39820
curl -s localhost:39820/goal -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"goal":"...","max":16}'

Record, Replay, And Reports

tsaagan record_start name=weekly-report
tsaagan goto url=https://example.com expectText="Example"
tsaagan click ref=e6 expectText="IANA"
tsaagan record_stop                         # writes ~/.tsaagan/records/*.json
tsaagan replay path=/Users/me/.tsaagan/records/...
tsaagan report format=md                    # writes ~/.tsaagan/reports/*.md

Recording captures successful actions with structural verify evidence and stores stable role/name targets when a step was originally addressed by an a11y ref, so replays are less tied to one transient browser snapshot. Reports summarize actions, failures, verification blocks, failed requests, and human-handoff moments for audit or debugging.

Diagnostics And Protocol

tsaagan doctor                              # local install/config health check
tsaagan protocol                            # paths to protocol/actions.schema.json + protocol/tsaagan.d.ts

doctor does not start a browser; it checks local prerequisites, extension files, vault backend availability, daemon reachability, and shared-host token posture.

Grounding priority

ref (from a fresh snapshot) → selector (stable CSS) → text → som (vision). Never coordinates.

Modes

  • fresh (default) — isolated real Chrome (channel: chrome) with its own profile. CDP.
  • clone / live — connect over CDP to a Chrome started with --remote-debugging-port=9222.
  • native (macOS) — real Chrome, no CDP (AppleScript + OS-level input). For sites that ignore synthetic input. See "Reliability" above.
  • extension (any OS) — Chrome via a companion extension (chrome.debugger Input → isTrusted=true, viewport coords, no debug port, no coordinate math). One command (ext-setup), zero manual steps; E2E-verified — a page-side listener reads event.isTrusted === true. The most reliable mode on sites that ignore synthetic input. See docs/EXTENSION.md.

Environment

Brain (autonomous mode, pick one): a provider key — GROQ_API_KEY · OPENROUTER_API_KEY · OPENAI_API_KEY · GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY · ANTHROPIC_API_KEY · XAI_API_KEY/GROK_API_KEY — or TSG_LLM_BASE_URL (+ TSG_LLM_API_KEY) for any OpenAI-compatible endpoint · TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai to name one · model overrides TSG_PLANNER_MODEL / TSG_NAV_MODEL / TSG_VALIDATOR_MODEL (legacy GROQ_*_MODEL still honored). Other: TSG_USER / TSG_PASS / TSG_TOTP_SECRET (login) · TSG_PORT (daemon port) · TSG_EMBED_URL (optional embedding endpoint for semantic recall). State lives in ~/.tsaagan/.

Architecture

tsaagan.js  ── thin CLI ──▶  daemon.js  (one persistent Playwright page; HTTP control plane)
                                  │   perceive · ground · act · VERIFY · self-heal
agent.js   ── planner→navigator→validator loop (LLM-agnostic via lib/llm.js) + task memory
run.js     ── one-shot autonomous run        server.js ── standalone HTTP goal server
bench.js   ── capability benchmark

The daemon holds a single long-lived page so accessibility-tree refs stay valid across discrete CLI calls, and verification/self-heal run server-side with full page state.

Input modes — and why trusted input matters

Tsaagan has three ways to drive the browser. The reason for more than one is purely correctness: a DOM event has an isTrusted flag that's true only for genuine user input, and some well-built sites ignore synthetic clicks/keystrokes for their own integrity — so your authorized automation silently does nothing. Native and extension modes deliver isTrusted=true input so your agent's actions actually take effect.

CDP mode (default) — Playwright driving Chrome. Fast, full-featured, parallel, works on the vast majority of the web (dashboards, SaaS, internal tools). Uses the real Chrome binary. Input here is isTrusted=false, so the rare strict site ignores it — that's what the other two modes are for.

Native mode (macOS) — tsaagan start mode=native. Drives your real Chrome with no debug port: perception via AppleScript, input via real OS-level mouse/keyboard (cliclick) → isTrusted=true. Trade-offs: macOS-only · slower · single front window · coordinate/vision grounding. Needs brew install cliclick + Chrome ▸ View ▸ Developer ▸ "Allow JavaScript from Apple Events".

Extension mode (any OS) — tsaagan ext-setup. A companion extension delivers isTrusted=true clicks at viewport coordinates via chrome.debugger — the cleanest trusted-input path. See docs/EXTENSION.md.

brew install cliclick                       # one-time
tsaagan start mode=native                   # drives your real, frontmost Chrome
tsaagan snapshot                            # AppleScript perception (elements + screen coords)
tsaagan click i=5                           # real OS-level trusted click
tsaagan paste text="..." submit=true        # clipboard + real ⌘V + Return

Self-learning & memory

Tsaagan keeps a small, local memory under ~/.tsaagan/:

  • Per-site memory (memory/<domain>.json) — working selectors and notes. Tsaagan auto-captures a selector when an action succeeds (no manual step), and you can also teach it explicitly with remember / recall / forget.
  • Auto-learned lessons — if a site shows an anti-abuse / CAPTCHA / "unusual activity" wall, Tsaagan automatically writes a note to that domain and flips it to pace: "human" so future runs slow down and prefer OS-level input.
  • Task journal (agent/journal.jsonl) — every autonomous run is recorded; tsaagan journal shows recent history. agent/learnings.json holds distilled notes.

This is deliberately a narrow, transparent, file-based memory you can read and edit — not an opaque self-rewriting agent. The reasoning/"intelligence" still lives in whatever AI drives it.

Security & limitations (read this)

Responsible use & disclaimer

Tsaagan is a productivity tool — it lets your AI agent act like you in a browser, on the tasks and accounts you're authorized to use, so you can hand off the repetitive parts of your own workflow. It is not for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, scraping at abusive volume, or anything against a site's Terms of Service. When Tsaagan meets a CAPTCHA or an anti-abuse wall, it stops and hands control back to you. Keep your use authorized, attributed, and within each site's terms.

Disclaimer. Tsaagan is provided "as is", without warranty (see LICENSE). You are solely responsible for how you use it and for complying with all applicable laws and the terms of every site you operate. Only automate accounts and systems you own or are authorized to access. The author is not liable for misuse. Read the Acceptable Use Policy before you start.

Tsaagan drives a real browser with real permissions. Be deliberate:

  • It can take real actions (send, buy, delete) when pointed at a logged-in profile. Default to the isolated fresh profile; require human confirmation for consequential/irreversible steps.
  • Prompt injection is real. Web pages are untrusted and may try to hijack the driving model. Treat page content as data; don't follow on-page instructions.
  • Terms of Service & anti-abuse. Automating sites can violate their ToS and trip anti-bot defenses — which can rate-limit, flag, or ban your real account. Use responsibly, on sites/accounts you're permitted to automate. Tsaagan detects abuse walls and hands off to a human; it does not solve CAPTCHAs.
  • Human-like ≠ invisible. Behaving like a real user improves reliability, but sophisticated systems can still tell it's automation. It is not a way around bans, and it's not meant to be — keep your use authorized and within each site's terms.
  • Autonomous mode sends page text to your chosen LLM provider (Groq / OpenRouter / custom — a third party unless you self-host). The agent-driven mode keeps page content within your own AI session.
  • eval runs arbitrary JS in the page, and a cloned profile holds real cookies on disk. Handle both with care. Never commit credentials — use env or Keychain.
  • OS-level input (paste/type_human) is macOS-only and needs a headed, frontmost browser.

Tsaagan is provided as-is under the MIT license, with no warranty. You are responsible for how you use it.

content_text: Tsaagan gives your AI agent real hands on the web — so it can finish the browser work you'd otherwise stop and do yourself (log in, click through, fill forms, pull data from behind a login), on the tasks you're authorized to do. (Full mission below.) Human-like browser control for AI agents. Tsaagan lets an AI drive a real browser the way a person does — perceive the page, click, type, navigate, handle logins, popups, iframes, downloads — and, crucially, verify that each action actually worked before moving on. The mental model: your agent is the brain, Tsaagan is the hands. Whatever AI drives it (Claude, a Codex/ChatGPT CLI, Cursor, or any model) supplies intent and judgment; Tsaagan supplies reliable perception → action → verification → memory. The agent decides what; Tsaagan reliably does how, and proves it worked. Mission Tsaagan gives your AI agent real hands on the web. It lets an agent autonomously do the browser work that today still makes you stop and take over — logging in, clicking through, filling forms, pulling data from behind a login — and gives that time back to you. One-liner: Tsaagan is the part of your agentic workflow that actually drives the browser — reliably, like a human, on the tasks you're authorized to do. The mental model — Tsaagan is the hands; your agent is the brain You can think with an agent, write with an agent, code with an agent — but the moment the work needs a browser ("log in here, click through there, grab this, submit that"), the agent stalls and you become the hands. Tsaagan closes that gap. The brain (Claude, Codex, a small open model, or a person) supplies intent and judgment; Tsaagan supplies reliable perception → action → verification → memory. Three ideas make it click: It hovers, then strikes. Like the bird — patient perception, then one exact move. It reads the page's accessibility tree, picks the right element, acts, and then proves the action worked from real page state (URL changed? expected text? errors?) instead of clicking and hoping. That verify step is the difference between "automation that mostly works" and automation you can trust unattended. Lightest tool that does the job. If a site has an API, use the API. If not, drive the real browser like a human (trusted input). Only fall back to raw automation for the broad middle of the web. It remembers and improves. It keeps an inspectable, file-based memory of the sites it works — learned selectors, what went wrong last time — so it gets better at your recurring tasks. What it does for the user Turns "every week I log into X, click through, and copy Y" into "my agent does it and reports back what it verified." Works on real, logged-in sites — dashboards, portals, tools with no API — where a scraper or WebFetch can't go. Keeps you in control: it stops and hands back at CAPTCHAs, and flags consequential, irreversible actions (buy, send, delete). Empowerment, not autopilot-into-a-wall. What Tsaagan deliberately is not Tsaagan is not a tool for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, or doing anything against a site's Terms of Service. It is for builders and creators who want their agents to finish the job on work they're already authorized to do — and it's honest about its limits rather than overselling. Only automate accounts and systems you own or are authorized to access. When it meets a CAPTCHA or an anti-abuse wall, it stops and hands control back to you. See the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer — you are solely responsible for how you use it. Philosophy — why I built Tsaagan I kept hitting the same wall in my own agentic workflows: you can reason, write, and code with an agent — but the moment a task needs a browser (log in, click through, pull this, file that), the agent stalls and a human has to take over. That handoff is where the time goes. Tsaagan exists to give your AI agents real hands — so they can autonomously do that web work and give the time back to you. It's for builders and creators who want their agents to actually finish the job, not just plan it. And it's built on a clear ethic: empowerment, not abuse. Tsaagan deliberately does not defeat CAPTCHAs, asks for human confirmation on consequential actions, stores secrets only in your OS keychain, keeps everything local, and respects site Terms of Service. It exists to extend what you can build — not to create risk. That ethic is encoded in SOUL.md and enforced in AGENTS.md. Use it on tasks you're authorized to do. Tsaagan is for automating your own accounts and authorized work — not for unauthorized access or circumventing security. Please read the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer. — Michael Olmos, creator Capability layers — use the lightest one that does the job API layer — if a site has an official API, Tsaagan calls it directly with a key kept in your Keychain (fastest and most reliable). The browser layers can even set the key up for you. → docs/API.md Real-browser layer — extension (any OS) / native (macOS): drives your real Chrome with trusted input, no debug port — for sites that ignore synthetic input. → docs/EXTENSION.md CDP layer — Playwright on the real Chrome binary for the broad middle of the web. Plus an agent layer (identity + brain memory) that makes Tsaagan a self-improving agent, not just a tool. → docs/AGENT.md Memory that learns (a real loop, not just storage) Tsaagan doesn't just remember — it learns from each run and feeds the lesson back: Reflect: a successful run is stored as a reusable procedure; a failure becomes a one-line lesson (via an LLM reflection); an anti-abuse wall flips the domain to pace: human. Accumulated lessons are synthesized into durable rules. Feed back: every snapshot carries a memory block (rules, lessons, pace, success track) — so the driving agent (Claude, Codex, or any LLM) adapts before it acts. Research-grade recall: ranked by relevance (embeddings) × importance × recency, not substring match. Zero-dependency by default; real neural embeddings if TSG_EMBED_URL or @xenova/transformers is present. Look-ahead + evaluator gate: it imagines the outcome before irreversible actions, and only stores a procedure when success is validator- and structurally-verified. Memory lives on your machine (~/.tsaagan/brain.db) — not in a model, not in the cloud. → docs/AGENT.md Most browser-agent stacks fail for the same two reasons: they ground actions on ephemeral element ids that break the moment the page changes, and they trust the model's guess that an action succeeded. Tsaagan fixes both: Stable, self-healing grounding. Elements are addressed by accessibility-tree refs; if a ref goes stale, Tsaagan re-snapshots and re-locates the same element by role + name automatically. Structural verification. After every action Tsaagan returns a verify block built from real page state — URL delta, expected text, new console errors, failed network requests — so the agent knows whether it worked. It's three things in one: A CLI + daemon you (or a coding agent) drive verb-by-verb. A library (agent.js) with an autonomous planner→navigator→validator loop. A standalone server you hand goals to over HTTP — no agent framework required. Why Tsaagan Tsaagan Perception Accessibility tree (primary) + vision Set-of-Marks fallback for canvas/visual UIs Grounding Stable refs → self-heal on staleness. Never raw coordinates, never brittle XPath as primary Verification Structural post-conditions every action (URL / text / console / network) Hard cases iframes, shadow DOM, popups, cookie banners, file upload/download, multi-tab Memory Learns each site — durable selectors persisted per domain, replayed across sessions Auth Cloned/real Chrome profile, one-shot login, TOTP 2FA (RFC 6238) Trusted input isTrusted=true clicks/keystrokes via native (macOS) / extension modes — for sites that ignore synthetic input Autonomy Planner → navigator → validator loop; runs headless or as an HTTP goal server How Tsaagan compares The browser-agent space is crowded — and Tsaagan is the new, small project here. It doesn't try to out-scale browser-use or out-distribute Microsoft's playwright-mcp. It wins on a different axis: reliability you can audit. The one capability every other tool lacks — structural proof that each action worked — is Tsaagan's core. Capability Tsaagan browser-use stagehand playwright-mcp skyvern Verify-first (post-condition proof per action) ✅ core ❌ ❌ ❌ ⚠️ vision API-first layer (skip the browser when a site has an API) ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ Self-healing stable a11y refs ✅ ⚠️ ⚠️ ⚠️ ❌ OS-keychain vault + TOTP/2FA ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ ⚠️ cloud Cross-session site memory (semantic recall) ✅ ❌ ⚠️ ❌ ❌ MCP server ✅ ❌ ✅ ✅ ❌ Trusted input, no CDP debug port (native + MV3) ✅ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ Vision fallback (Set-of-Marks) ✅ ✅ ✅ ❌ ✅ Local / offline capable ✅ ✅ ❌ ✅ ❌ Model-agnostic (bring your own LLM) ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅ ⚠️ Language Node.js Python TypeScript TypeScript Python License MIT MIT MIT Apache-2.0 AGPL-3.0 Published WebVoyager score ⚠️ in progress ✅ ~89% ⚠️ ❌ ✅ ~86% GitHub stars (Jun 2026) early ~99k ~23k ~34k ~22k Cloud / hosted option ❌ self-host ⚠️ ❌ Browserbase ❌ ✅ ✅ supported · ⚠️ partial/workaround · ❌ not supported Honest notes. Tsaagan is young — fewer stars and no hosted option, and its benchmark number is still being run (published scores across tools use different WebVoyager task subsets and aren't directly comparable). What it uniquely offers: proof-of-success on every action, an API-first fast path, a local credential vault, and trusted-input modes that need no debug port. How it works — tool, skill, or agent? Tsaagan is one engine you can drive several ways: The tool — a CLI (tsaagan.js) talking to a background daemon that holds a single persistent Playwright page. This is the engine; everything else wraps it. As a skill — a short instruction file that teaches your AI how to drive the tool (the observe → act → verify loop). "Use the Tsaagan skill" → the AI reads it and drives Tsaagan step by step. (A ready-made skill for Claude Code is included.) As an agent — a dispatchable worker that bundles the skill + the tool, so you can hand it a goal and it runs the loop on its own. As an MCP server or SDK — tsaagan mcp exposes the verify-first verbs to Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor, and the JS + Python SDKs call the same daemon directly. (See Use it from Claude/Cursor (MCP) and Use it from your own code above.) Who is the "brain"? Whatever drives it: You drive it with… Brain API key needed Claude Code (the skill/agent) your Claude session none — your Claude plan Codex / ChatGPT CLI, Cursor, etc. that tool's model that tool's tsaagan run / tsaagan serve (autonomous) any OpenAI-compatible LLM — Groq · OpenRouter · OpenAI · Google Gemini · Anthropic · xAI Grok · local a key for your chosen provider Tsaagan itself makes no LLM calls when an agent drives it — it's pure perception + action + verification. Only the autonomous tiers call an LLM, and that LLM is your choice: Groq (default, fast), OpenRouter for open-source models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral), the enterprise vendors — OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic, xAI Grok (each via its OpenAI-compatible endpoint) — or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Together, vLLM, Ollama for fully local) via TSG_LLM_BASE_URL. Pick one with TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai. See docs/AGENT.md. Use it with any coding agent Tsaagan is just a terminal program, so any agent that can run shell commands can drive it. Tell your agent something like: "Use Tsaagan (node tsaagan.js) to log into example.com and download my invoices. Snapshot, act on refs, and check the verify block after each step." It will run tsaagan start, tsaagan snapshot, tsaagan click ref=…, read the JSON results, and proceed. For Claude Code, the bundled skill/agent make this automatic. Install git clone https://github.com/michaelolmos/tsaagan.git && cd tsaagan npm install npx playwright install chromium chromium-headless-shell Quick start (drive it yourself) node tsaagan.js start # launch browser (fresh isolated profile) node tsaagan.js goto url=https://example.com expectText="Example" node tsaagan.js snapshot # accessibility tree with [ref=eN] node tsaagan.js click ref=e6 expectText="IANA" node tsaagan.js type ref=e3 text="hello" submit=true node tsaagan.js stop Add an alias: alias tsaagan="node $PWD/tsaagan.js". Use it from Claude Desktop, Claude Code, or Cursor (MCP) Tsaagan ships a built-in MCP server, so any Model Context Protocol host can drive the browser directly. Unlike other browser MCP servers, every mutating tool returns Tsaagan's verify block (URL changed? console errors? expected text?) — proof the action worked, in the same response, with no extra snapshot round-trip. Claude Code: claude mcp add tsaagan -- node /path/to/tsaagan/tsaagan.js mcp # or, if you've installed the `tsaagan` bin globally: claude mcp add tsaagan -- tsaagan mcp Claude Desktop (claude_desktop_config.json) or Cursor (~/.cursor/mcp.json): { "mcpServers": { "tsaagan": { "command": "node", "args": ["/path/to/tsaagan/tsaagan.js", "mcp"] } } } The server auto-starts a headless daemon on first use. It exposes 21 verify-first tools — tsaagan_navigate, tsaagan_snapshot, tsaagan_click, tsaagan_fill_form, tsaagan_extract, tsaagan_network (discover a site's own data API), and more. Set TSAAGAN_HEADLESS=0 to watch it work, or TSG_TOKEN to lock the control plane on shared machines. See docs/PROTOCOL.md. Use it from your own code (JS & Python SDK) Prefer calling Tsaagan programmatically? Both clients return a verify block on every call — data and proof, together (the thing other tools make you assert yourself). JavaScript / TypeScript (sdk/, zero dependencies): import { createTsaagan } from 'tsaagan/sdk'; // or '/path/to/tsaagan/sdk/index.mjs' const k = await createTsaagan(); // auto-starts a headless daemon await k.goto('https://example.com', { expectText: 'Example Domain' }); const r = await k.extract('the page heading'); console.log(r.data, r.verify); // data + { urlChanged, expectTextFound, newConsoleErrors, ... } await k.stop(); Python (pip install tsaagan, zero dependencies): from tsaagan import Tsaagan k = Tsaagan() # auto-starts a headless daemon k.goto("https://example.com", expect_text="Example Domain") r = k.extract("the page heading") print(r.data, r.verify) k.stop() The Python client needs the Node daemon reachable — set TSAAGAN_JS=/path/to/tsaagan.js or put the tsaagan binary on PATH. See clients/python/. Autonomous mode export GROQ_API_KEY=... # the autonomous brain (fast default) # …or any other provider — name it and supply that provider's key: export OPENROUTER_API_KEY=... TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter # open-source models export OPENAI_API_KEY=... TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=openai # GPT-4o / o-series export GEMINI_API_KEY=... TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=google # Gemini export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic # Claude export XAI_API_KEY=... TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=xai # Grok export TSG_LLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions # Ollama, fully local node tsaagan.js run goal="go to news.ycombinator.com and report the #1 story title" node tsaagan.js journal # what it has done before (persistent memory) Per-role model overrides work with any provider: TSG_PLANNER_MODEL, TSG_NAV_MODEL, TSG_VALIDATOR_MODEL (e.g. point the planner at a stronger model for reasoning while a fast model handles navigation). Standalone server (no agent framework) node tsaagan.js serve port=39820 curl -s localhost:39820/goal -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"goal":"...","max":16}' Benchmark node tsaagan.js bench # autonomous task suite + deterministic capability matrix Latest run (Groq brain, 4-task suite): Metric Result Task success 3/4 (75%) Self-heal (recover from stale refs) ✅ pass Vision Set-of-Marks ✅ pass Structural verify (post-conditions) ✅ pass A directly-comparable WebVoyager score is in progress — see bench/METHODOLOGY.md for exactly how it will be run and reported honestly (homepage-start, no post-hoc judge overrides, published task set). Command reference Command Description start [mode=fresh|clone|live] [headless=true] [port=] launch / attach a browser status · stop state · shut down snapshot [full=true] · snapshot mode=vision a11y tree with refs · numbered visual overlay goto url=.. [expectText=..] navigate + verify click ref=eN|som=N|selector=..|text=.. [key=..] [expectText=..] [expectGone=..] [expectUrl=..] click + verify + self-heal type ref=eN text=".." [key=..] [submit=true] [expectText=..] clear + type select · press keys=".." [expectText=..] · scroll [direction=|to_text=] · wait_for [..] interaction upload_file ref=|selector= path=a,b · handle_dialog accept=true · dismiss_overlays · downloads hard cases extract [query=..] · screenshot [path=] [fullPage=true] read page tabs · switch_tab index=N · new_tab url=.. · close_tab · back · forward tabs / history remember key=.. selector=.. [note=..] · recall · forget learn a site (per-domain memory) login userSelector=.. passSelector=.. [submitSelector=..] [totpSelector=.. totpSecret=..] one-shot login + 2FA totp secret=$TSG_TOTP_SECRET · detect_captcha 2FA code · detect a CAPTCHA/anti-abuse wall → hand off to a human paste text=".." [ref=|selector=] [submit=true] · type_human · key_human keys="return" OS-level input (macOS) — trusted (isTrusted=true) keystrokes/paste for sites that ignore synthetic input pace set=human|slow|fast · click_xy x= y= · keychain service= account= cadence governor · coordinate click · macOS Keychain secret record_start name=.. · record_stop [path=..] · replay path=.. · report [format=json|md] record/replay verified sequences · export audit reports doctor · protocol local diagnostics · JSON Schema / TypeScript action protocol paths run goal=".." [max=16] · serve [port=] · journal [n=] · bench autonomy network [filter=] [limit=] captured XHR/fetch + responses (discover a site's internal API) cookies [set=<json>] export / import cookies (session reuse) pdf [path=] · assert [text=] [url=] [selectorVisible=] save page PDF · explicit verification check eval js=".." · console_log [limit=] escape hatch · debugging Start flags also include proxy=http://user:pass@host:port (e.g. a corporate proxy), channel=chrome\|chromium, pace=fast\|slow\|human (politeness), timezone=. Layer & agent commands Command Description vault set service=.. secret=.. [account=..] · vault get/list/delete Keychain-encrypted key store (secrets never hit disk) api service=.. path=/.. [method=] [body=] · api detect=<url> · api providers call an official API with a stored key (Layer 1) ext-setup [profile=fresh|clone] [browser=chrome] zero-step extension mode: launches Chrome for Testing with the companion extension auto-loaded + starts mode=extension. (browser=chrome targets your branded Chrome instead — needs a one-time manual load, since Chrome 137+ blocks --load-extension.) brain · brain recall query=.. · brain advise domain=.. · brain synthesize domain=.. evolving SQLite memory: stats · semantic recall · what-it-knows · compress lessons→rules advise [domain=..] [query=..] what Tsaagan learned about a site (also auto-surfaced in every snapshot.memory) journal [n=] recent autonomous-run history Record, Replay, And Reports tsaagan record_start name=weekly-report tsaagan goto url=https://example.com expectText="Example" tsaagan click ref=e6 expectText="IANA" tsaagan record_stop # writes ~/.tsaagan/records/*.json tsaagan replay path=/Users/me/.tsaagan/records/... tsaagan report format=md # writes ~/.tsaagan/reports/*.md Recording captures successful actions with structural verify evidence and stores stable role/name targets when a step was originally addressed by an a11y ref, so replays are less tied to one transient browser snapshot. Reports summarize actions, failures, verification blocks, failed requests, and human-handoff moments for audit or debugging. Diagnostics And Protocol tsaagan doctor # local install/config health check tsaagan protocol # paths to protocol/actions.schema.json + protocol/tsaagan.d.ts doctor does not start a browser; it checks local prerequisites, extension files, vault backend availability, daemon reachability, and shared-host token posture. Grounding priority ref (from a fresh snapshot) → selector (stable CSS) → text → som (vision). Never coordinates. Modes fresh (default) — isolated real Chrome (channel: chrome) with its own profile. CDP. clone / live — connect over CDP to a Chrome started with --remote-debugging-port=9222. native (macOS) — real Chrome, no CDP (AppleScript + OS-level input). For sites that ignore synthetic input. See "Reliability" above. extension (any OS) — Chrome via a companion extension (chrome.debugger Input → isTrusted=true, viewport coords, no debug port, no coordinate math). One command (ext-setup), zero manual steps; E2E-verified — a page-side listener reads event.isTrusted === true. The most reliable mode on sites that ignore synthetic input. See docs/EXTENSION.md. Environment Brain (autonomous mode, pick one): a provider key — GROQ_API_KEY · OPENROUTER_API_KEY · OPENAI_API_KEY · GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY · ANTHROPIC_API_KEY · XAI_API_KEY/GROK_API_KEY — or TSG_LLM_BASE_URL (+ TSG_LLM_API_KEY) for any OpenAI-compatible endpoint · TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai to name one · model overrides TSG_PLANNER_MODEL / TSG_NAV_MODEL / TSG_VALIDATOR_MODEL (legacy GROQ_*_MODEL still honored). Other: TSG_USER / TSG_PASS / TSG_TOTP_SECRET (login) · TSG_PORT (daemon port) · TSG_EMBED_URL (optional embedding endpoint for semantic recall). State lives in ~/.tsaagan/. Architecture tsaagan.js ── thin CLI ──▶ daemon.js (one persistent Playwright page; HTTP control plane) │ perceive · ground · act · VERIFY · self-heal agent.js ── planner→navigator→validator loop (LLM-agnostic via lib/llm.js) + task memory run.js ── one-shot autonomous run server.js ── standalone HTTP goal server bench.js ── capability benchmark The daemon holds a single long-lived page so accessibility-tree refs stay valid across discrete CLI calls, and verification/self-heal run server-side with full page state. Input modes — and why trusted input matters Tsaagan has three ways to drive the browser. The reason for more than one is purely correctness: a DOM event has an isTrusted flag that's true only for genuine user input, and some well-built sites ignore synthetic clicks/keystrokes for their own integrity — so your authorized automation silently does nothing. Native and extension modes deliver isTrusted=true input so your agent's actions actually take effect. CDP mode (default) — Playwright driving Chrome. Fast, full-featured, parallel, works on the vast majority of the web (dashboards, SaaS, internal tools). Uses the real Chrome binary. Input here is isTrusted=false, so the rare strict site ignores it — that's what the other two modes are for. Native mode (macOS) — tsaagan start mode=native. Drives your real Chrome with no debug port: perception via AppleScript, input via real OS-level mouse/keyboard (cliclick) → isTrusted=true. Trade-offs: macOS-only · slower · single front window · coordinate/vision grounding. Needs brew install cliclick + Chrome ▸ View ▸ Developer ▸ "Allow JavaScript from Apple Events". Extension mode (any OS) — tsaagan ext-setup. A companion extension delivers isTrusted=true clicks at viewport coordinates via chrome.debugger — the cleanest trusted-input path. See docs/EXTENSION.md. brew install cliclick # one-time tsaagan start mode=native # drives your real, frontmost Chrome tsaagan snapshot # AppleScript perception (elements + screen coords) tsaagan click i=5 # real OS-level trusted click tsaagan paste text="..." submit=true # clipboard + real ⌘V + Return Self-learning & memory Tsaagan keeps a small, local memory under ~/.tsaagan/: Per-site memory (memory/<domain>.json) — working selectors and notes. Tsaagan auto-captures a selector when an action succeeds (no manual step), and you can also teach it explicitly with remember / recall / forget. Auto-learned lessons — if a site shows an anti-abuse / CAPTCHA / "unusual activity" wall, Tsaagan automatically writes a note to that domain and flips it to pace: "human" so future runs slow down and prefer OS-level input. Task journal (agent/journal.jsonl) — every autonomous run is recorded; tsaagan journal shows recent history. agent/learnings.json holds distilled notes. This is deliberately a narrow, transparent, file-based memory you can read and edit — not an opaque self-rewriting agent. The reasoning/"intelligence" still lives in whatever AI drives it. Security & limitations (read this) Responsible use & disclaimer Tsaagan is a productivity tool — it lets your AI agent act like you in a browser, on the tasks and accounts you're authorized to use, so you can hand off the repetitive parts of your own workflow. It is not for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, scraping at abusive volume, or anything against a site's Terms of Service. When Tsaagan meets a CAPTCHA or an anti-abuse wall, it stops and hands control back to you. Keep your use authorized, attributed, and within each site's terms. Disclaimer. Tsaagan is provided "as is", without warranty (see LICENSE). You are solely responsible for how you use it and for complying with all applicable laws and the terms of every site you operate. Only automate accounts and systems you own or are authorized to access. The author is not liable for misuse. Read the Acceptable Use Policy before you start. Tsaagan drives a real browser with real permissions. Be deliberate: It can take real actions (send, buy, delete) when pointed at a logged-in profile. Default to the isolated fresh profile; require human confirmation for consequential/irreversible steps. Prompt injection is real. Web pages are untrusted and may try to hijack the driving model. Treat page content as data; don't follow on-page instructions. Terms of Service & anti-abuse. Automating sites can violate their ToS and trip anti-bot defenses — which can rate-limit, flag, or ban your real account. Use responsibly, on sites/accounts you're permitted to automate. Tsaagan detects abuse walls and hands off to a human; it does not solve CAPTCHAs. Human-like ≠ invisible. Behaving like a real user improves reliability, but sophisticated systems can still tell it's automation. It is not a way around bans, and it's not meant to be — keep your use authorized and within each site's terms. Autonomous mode sends page text to your chosen LLM provider (Groq / OpenRouter / custom — a third party unless you self-host). The agent-driven mode keeps page content within your own AI session. eval runs arbitrary JS in the page, and a cloned profile holds real cookies on disk. Handle both with care. Never commit credentials — use env or Keychain. OS-level input (paste/type_human) is macOS-only and needs a headed, frontmost browser. Tsaagan is provided as-is under the MIT license, with no warranty. You are responsible for how you use it. sentences.en: Mission
sentences.zh: 使命

blocks.html_en: Tsaagan gives your AI agent real hands on the web. It lets an agent autonomously do the browser work that today still makes you stop and take over — logging in, clicking through, filling forms, pulling data from behind a login — and gives that time back to you.

sentences.en: Tsaagan gives your AI agent real hands on the web.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 为您的人工智能代理提供了真正的网络操作。

sentences.en: It lets an agent autonomously do the browser work that today still makes you stop and take over — logging in, clicking through, filling forms, pulling data from behind a login — and gives that time back to you.
sentences.zh: 它可以让代理自主地完成浏览器的工作，而这些工作在今天仍然需要您停下来并接管——登录、点击、填写表格、从登录后提取数据——并将这些时间还给您。

blocks.html_en: One-liner: Tsaagan is the part of your agentic workflow that actually drives the browser — reliably, like a human, on the tasks you're authorized to do.

sentences.en: One-liner: Tsaagan is the part of your agentic workflow that actually drives the browser — reliably, like a human, on the tasks you're authorized to do.
sentences.zh: 一句话：Tsaagan 是代理工作流程的一部分，它实际上驱动浏览器——像人类一样可靠地执行您有权执行的任务。

blocks.html_en: The mental model — Tsaagan is the hands; your agent is the brain

sentences.en: The mental model — Tsaagan is the hands; your agent is the brain
sentences.zh: 心智模型——Tsaagan 是双手；你的代理人是大脑

blocks.html_en: You can think with an agent, write with an agent, code with an agent — but the moment the work needs a browser ("log in here, click through there, grab this, submit that"), the agent stalls and you become the hands. Tsaagan closes that gap. The brain (Claude, Codex, a small open model, or a person) supplies intent and judgment; Tsaagan supplies reliable perception → action → verification → memory.

sentences.en: You can think with an agent, write with an agent, code with an agent — but the moment the work needs a browser ("log in here, click through there, grab this, submit that"), the agent stalls and you become the hands.
sentences.zh: 你可以与代理一起思考、与代理一起编写、与代理一起编码——但是当工作需要浏览器时（“在这里登录，在那里点击，抓取这个，提交那个”），代理就会停滞不前，你就成为了手。

sentences.en: Tsaagan closes that gap.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 缩小了这一差距。

sentences.en: The brain (Claude, Codex, a small open model, or a person) supplies intent and judgment; Tsaagan supplies reliable perception → action → verification → memory.
sentences.zh: 大脑（Claude、Codex、小型开放模型或人）提供意图和判断； Tsaagan 提供可靠的感知→行动→验证→记忆。

sentences.en: Three ideas make it click:
sentences.zh: 三个想法让它点击：

blocks.html_en: • It hovers, then strikes. Like the bird — patient perception, then one exact move. It reads the page's accessibility tree, picks the right element, acts, and then proves the action worked from real page state (URL changed? expected text? errors?) instead of clicking and hoping. That verify step is the difference between "automation that mostly works" and automation you can trust unattended.

sentences.en: It hovers, then strikes.
sentences.zh: 它盘旋，然后攻击。

sentences.en: Like the bird — patient perception, then one exact move.
sentences.zh: 就像鸟一样——耐心感知，然后采取准确的行动。

sentences.en: It reads the page's accessibility tree, picks the right element, acts, and then proves the action worked from real page state (URL changed?
sentences.zh: 它读取页面的可访问性树，选择正确的元素，执行操作，然后证明该操作在真实页面状态下有效（URL 已更改？

sentences.en: expected text?
sentences.zh: 预期文本？

sentences.en: errors?)
sentences.zh: 错误？）

sentences.en: instead of clicking and hoping.
sentences.zh: 而不是点击和希望。

sentences.en: That verify step is the difference between "automation that mostly works" and automation you can trust unattended.
sentences.zh: 该验证步骤是“大部分有效的自动化”和您可以信任的无人值守自动化之间的区别。

blocks.html_en: • Lightest tool that does the job. If a site has an API, use the API. If not, drive the real browser like a human (trusted input). Only fall back to raw automation for the broad middle of the web.

sentences.en: Lightest tool that does the job.
sentences.zh: 完成这项工作的最轻工具。

sentences.en: If a site has an API, use the API.
sentences.zh: 如果网站有 API，请使用该 API。

sentences.en: If not, drive the real browser like a human (trusted input).
sentences.zh: 如果没有，请像人类一样驱动真正的浏览器（可信输入）。

sentences.en: Only fall back to raw automation for the broad middle of the web.
sentences.zh: 对于网络的广泛中间部分，只能回退到原始自动化。

blocks.html_en: • It remembers and improves. It keeps an inspectable, file-based memory of the sites it works — learned selectors, what went wrong last time — so it gets better at your recurring tasks.

sentences.en: It remembers and improves.
sentences.zh: 它会记住并改进。

sentences.en: It keeps an inspectable, file-based memory of the sites it works — learned selectors, what went wrong last time — so it gets better at your recurring tasks.
sentences.zh: 它对其工作的站点保留可检查的、基于文件的记忆——学习的选择器、上次出错的地方——因此它可以更好地完成您的重复任务。

blocks.html_en: What it does for the user

sentences.en: What it does for the user
sentences.zh: 它为用户做什么

blocks.html_en: • Turns "every week I log into X, click through, and copy Y" into "my agent does it and reports back what it verified."

sentences.en: Turns "every week I log into X, click through, and copy Y" into "my agent does it and reports back what it verified."
sentences.zh: 将“我每周登录 X、点击并复制 Y”变成“我的代理执行此操作并报告其验证的内容”。

blocks.html_en: • Works on real, logged-in sites — dashboards, portals, tools with no API — where a scraper or WebFetch can't go.

sentences.en: Works on real, logged-in sites — dashboards, portals, tools with no API — where a scraper or WebFetch can't go.
sentences.zh: 适用于真实的登录网站（仪表板、门户、没有 API 的工具），而爬虫或 WebFetch 无法访问这些网站。

blocks.html_en: • Keeps you in control: it stops and hands back at CAPTCHAs, and flags consequential, irreversible actions (buy, send, delete). Empowerment, not autopilot-into-a-wall.

sentences.en: Keeps you in control: it stops and hands back at CAPTCHAs, and flags consequential, irreversible actions (buy, send, delete).
sentences.zh: 让您保持掌控：它会停止并返回验证码，并标记相应的、不可逆转的操作（购买、发送、删除）。

sentences.en: Empowerment, not autopilot-into-a-wall.
sentences.zh: 授权，而不是自动驾驶撞墙。

sentences.en: What Tsaagan deliberately is not
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 故意的不是

blocks.html_en: Tsaagan is not a tool for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, or doing anything against a site's Terms of Service. It is for builders and creators who want their agents to finish the job on work they're already authorized to do — and it's honest about its limits rather than overselling. Only automate accounts and systems you own or are authorized to access. When it meets a CAPTCHA or an anti-abuse wall, it stops and hands control back to you. See the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer — you are solely responsible for how you use it.

sentences.en: Tsaagan is not a tool for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, or doing anything against a site's Terms of Service.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 不是用于破坏安全、解决验证码、逃避禁令或执行任何违反网站服务条款的行为的工具。

sentences.en: It is for builders and creators who want their agents to finish the job on work they're already authorized to do — and it's honest about its limits rather than overselling.
sentences.zh: 它是为那些希望他们的代理人完成他们已经被授权做的工作的建设者和创造者而设计的——而且它诚实地对待它的局限性，而不是过度推销。

sentences.en: Only automate accounts and systems you own or are authorized to access.
sentences.zh: 仅自动化您拥有或有权访问的帐户和系统。

sentences.en: When it meets a CAPTCHA or an anti-abuse wall, it stops and hands control back to you.
sentences.zh: 当它遇到验证码或反滥用墙时，它会停止并将控制权交还给您。

sentences.en: See the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer — you are solely responsible for how you use it.
sentences.zh: 请参阅可接受的使用政策和免责声明 - 您对如何使用它承担全部责任。

blocks.html_en: Philosophy — why I built Tsaagan

sentences.en: Philosophy — why I built Tsaagan
sentences.zh: 理念——我为何建造 Tsaagan

blocks.html_en: I kept hitting the same wall in my own agentic workflows: you can reason, write, and code with an agent — but the moment a task needs a browser (log in, click through, pull this, file that), the agent stalls and a human has to take over. That handoff is where the time goes.

sentences.en: I kept hitting the same wall in my own agentic workflows: you can reason, write, and code with an agent — but the moment a task needs a browser (log in, click through, pull this, file that), the agent stalls and a human has to take over.
sentences.zh: 我在自己的代理工作流程中不断遇到同样的问题：你可以使用代理进行推理、编写和编码，但是当任务需要浏览器时（登录、点击、拉取这个、归档那个），代理就会停止工作，而人类必须接管。

sentences.en: That handoff is where the time goes.
sentences.zh: 交接就是时间流逝的地方。

blocks.html_en: Tsaagan exists to give your AI agents real hands — so they can autonomously do that web work and give the time back to you. It's for builders and creators who want their agents to actually finish the job, not just plan it.

sentences.en: Tsaagan exists to give your AI agents real hands — so they can autonomously do that web work and give the time back to you.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 的存在是为了给你的人工智能代理真正的帮助——这样他们就可以自主地完成网络工作，并将时间还给你。

sentences.en: It's for builders and creators who want their agents to actually finish the job, not just plan it.
sentences.zh: 它适用于希望他们的代理实际完成工作而不仅仅是计划工作的建设者和创造者。

blocks.html_en: And it's built on a clear ethic: empowerment, not abuse. Tsaagan deliberately does not defeat CAPTCHAs, asks for human confirmation on consequential actions, stores secrets only in your OS keychain, keeps everything local, and respects site Terms of Service. It exists to extend what you can build — not to create risk. That ethic is encoded in SOUL.md and enforced in AGENTS.md.

sentences.en: And it's built on a clear ethic: empowerment, not abuse.
sentences.zh: 它建立在明确的道德基础上：授权，而不是滥用。

sentences.en: Tsaagan deliberately does not defeat CAPTCHAs, asks for human confirmation on consequential actions, stores secrets only in your OS keychain, keeps everything local, and respects site Terms of Service.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 故意不破坏验证码，要求对后续操作进行人工确认，仅将机密存储在您的操作系统钥匙串中，将所有内容保留在本地，并尊重网站服务条款。

sentences.en: It exists to extend what you can build — not to create risk.
sentences.zh: 它的存在是为了扩展你可以构建的东西，而不是创造风险。

sentences.en: That ethic is encoded in SOUL.md and enforced in AGENTS.md.
sentences.zh: 该道德规范被编码在 SOUL.md 中，并在 AGENTS.md 中强制执行。

blocks.html_en: Use it on tasks you're authorized to do. Tsaagan is for automating your own accounts and authorized work — not for unauthorized access or circumventing security. Please read the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer.

sentences.en: Use it on tasks you're authorized to do.
sentences.zh: 将其用于您有权执行的任务。

sentences.en: Tsaagan is for automating your own accounts and authorized work — not for unauthorized access or circumventing security.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 用于自动化您自己的帐户和授权工作，而不是用于未经授权的访问或规避安全性。

sentences.en: Please read the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer.
sentences.zh: 请阅读可接受的使用政策和免责声明。

blocks.html_en: Use it on tasks you're authorized to do. Tsaagan is for automating your own accounts and authorized work — not for unauthorized access or circumventing security. Please read the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer.

sentences.en: Use it on tasks you're authorized to do.
sentences.zh: 将其用于您有权执行的任务。

sentences.en: Please read the Acceptable Use Policy and the disclaimer.

sentences.en: — Michael Olmos, creator
sentences.zh: — 迈克尔·奥尔莫斯，创始人

blocks.html_en: Capability layers — use the lightest one that does the job

sentences.en: Capability layers — use the lightest one that does the job
sentences.zh: 能力层——使用最轻的能力层

blocks.html_en: • API layer — if a site has an official API, Tsaagan calls it directly with a key kept in your Keychain (fastest and most reliable). The browser layers can even set the key up for you. → docs/API.md

sentences.en: API layer — if a site has an official API, Tsaagan calls it directly with a key kept in your Keychain (fastest and most reliable).
sentences.zh: API 层 - 如果站点有官方 API，Tsaagan 可以使用钥匙串中保存的密钥直接调用它（最快且最可靠）。

sentences.en: The browser layers can even set the key up for you. → docs/API.md
sentences.zh: 浏览器层甚至可以为您设置密钥。 → 文档/API.md

blocks.html_en: • Real-browser layer — extension (any OS) / native (macOS): drives your real Chrome with trusted input, no debug port — for sites that ignore synthetic input. → docs/EXTENSION.md

sentences.en: Real-browser layer — extension (any OS) / native (macOS): drives your real Chrome with trusted input, no debug port — for sites that ignore synthetic input. → docs/EXTENSION.md
sentences.zh: 真实浏览器层 - 扩展（任何操作系统）/本机 (macOS)：使用可信输入驱动真正的 Chrome，无调试端口 - 适用于忽略合成输入的站点。 → 文档/扩展.md

blocks.html_en: • CDP layer — Playwright on the real Chrome binary for the broad middle of the web.

sentences.en: CDP layer — Playwright on the real Chrome binary for the broad middle of the web.
sentences.zh: CDP 层 — 真正的 Chrome 二进制文件的编剧，适用于网络的广泛中间层。

blocks.html_en: Plus an agent layer (identity + brain memory) that makes Tsaagan a self-improving agent, not just a tool. → docs/AGENT.md

sentences.en: Plus an agent layer (identity + brain memory) that makes Tsaagan a self-improving agent, not just a tool. → docs/AGENT.md
sentences.zh: 再加上代理层（身份+大脑记忆），使 Tsaagan 成为一个自我改进的代理，而不仅仅是一个工具。 → 文档/AGENT.md

blocks.html_en: Memory that learns (a real loop, not just storage)

sentences.en: Memory that learns (a real loop, not just storage)
sentences.zh: 学习的内存（真正的循环，而不仅仅是存储）

blocks.html_en: Tsaagan doesn't just remember — it learns from each run and feeds the lesson back:

sentences.en: Tsaagan doesn't just remember — it learns from each run and feeds the lesson back:
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 不仅能记住，还能从每次运行中学习并反馈教训：

blocks.html_en: • Reflect: a successful run is stored as a reusable procedure; a failure becomes a one-line lesson (via an LLM reflection); an anti-abuse wall flips the domain to pace: human. Accumulated lessons are synthesized into durable rules.

sentences.en: Reflect: a successful run is stored as a reusable procedure; a failure becomes a one-line lesson (via an LLM reflection); an anti-abuse wall flips the domain to pace: human.
sentences.zh: 反映：成功的运行被存储为可重用的过程；失败变成了一行教训（通过法学硕士反思）；反滥用墙将领域翻转为节奏：人类。

sentences.en: Accumulated lessons are synthesized into durable rules.
sentences.zh: 积累的经验教训被总结成持久的规则。

blocks.html_en: • Feed back: every snapshot carries a memory block (rules, lessons, pace, success track) — so the driving agent (Claude, Codex, or any LLM) adapts before it acts.

sentences.en: Feed back: every snapshot carries a memory block (rules, lessons, pace, success track) — so the driving agent (Claude, Codex, or any LLM) adapts before it acts.
sentences.zh: 反馈：每个快照都带有一个记忆块（规则、教训、节奏、成功轨迹）——因此驱动代理（Claude、Codex 或任何 LLM）在行动之前会进行调整。

blocks.html_en: • Research-grade recall: ranked by relevance (embeddings) × importance × recency, not substring match. Zero-dependency by default; real neural embeddings if TSG_EMBED_URL or @xenova/transformers is present.

sentences.en: Research-grade recall: ranked by relevance (embeddings) × importance × recency, not substring match.
sentences.zh: 研究级召回：按相关性（嵌入）×重要性×新近度排名，而不是子串匹配。

sentences.en: Zero-dependency by default; real neural embeddings if TSG_EMBED_URL or @xenova/transformers is present.
sentences.zh: 默认零依赖；如果存在 TSG_EMBED_URL 或 @xenova/transformers ，则为真正的神经嵌入。

blocks.html_en: • Look-ahead + evaluator gate: it imagines the outcome before irreversible actions, and only stores a procedure when success is validator- and structurally-verified.

sentences.en: Look-ahead + evaluator gate: it imagines the outcome before irreversible actions, and only stores a procedure when success is validator- and structurally-verified.
sentences.zh: 前瞻+评估器门：它在不可逆操作之前想象结果，并且仅在成功被验证器和结构验证时才存储过程。

blocks.html_en: Memory lives on your machine (~/.tsaagan/brain.db) — not in a model, not in the cloud. → docs/AGENT.md

sentences.en: Memory lives on your machine (~/.tsaagan/brain.db) — not in a model, not in the cloud. → docs/AGENT.md
sentences.zh: 内存位于您的机器上 (~/.tsaagan/brain.db) - 不在模型中，不在云中。 → 文档/AGENT.md

blocks.html_en: Most browser-agent stacks fail for the same two reasons: they ground actions on ephemeral element ids that break the moment the page changes, and they trust the model's guess that an action succeeded. Tsaagan fixes both:

sentences.en: Most browser-agent stacks fail for the same two reasons: they ground actions on ephemeral element ids that break the moment the page changes, and they trust the model's guess that an action succeeded.
sentences.zh: 大多数浏览器代理堆栈都会因同样的两个原因而失败：它们将操作基于临时元素 ID，这些操作会在页面更改时中断，并且它们相信模型对操作成功的猜测。

sentences.en: Tsaagan fixes both:
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 修复了以下两个问题：

blocks.html_en: • Stable, self-healing grounding. Elements are addressed by accessibility-tree refs; if a ref goes stale, Tsaagan re-snapshots and re-locates the same element by role + name automatically.

sentences.en: Stable, self-healing grounding.
sentences.zh: 稳定、自愈接地。

sentences.en: Elements are addressed by accessibility-tree refs; if a ref goes stale, Tsaagan re-snapshots and re-locates the same element by role + name automatically.
sentences.zh: 元素由可访问性树引用寻址；如果引用过时，Tsaagan 会自动重新快照并按角色 + 名称重新定位相同的元素。

blocks.html_en: • Structural verification. After every action Tsaagan returns a verify block built from real page state — URL delta, expected text, new console errors, failed network requests — so the agent knows whether it worked.

sentences.en: Structural verification.
sentences.zh: 结构验证。

sentences.en: After every action Tsaagan returns a verify block built from real page state — URL delta, expected text, new console errors, failed network requests — so the agent knows whether it worked.

blocks.html_en: Honest notes. Tsaagan is young — fewer stars and no hosted option, and its benchmark number is still being run (published scores across tools use different WebVoyager task subsets and aren't directly comparable). blocks.html_en: • The tool — a CLI (tsaagan.js) talking to a background daemon that holds a single persistent Playwright page. This is the engine; everything else wraps it.

blocks.html_en: • As a skill — a short instruction file that teaches your AI how to drive the tool (the observe → act → verify loop). "Use the Tsaagan skill" → the AI reads it and drives Tsaagan step by step. (A ready-made skill for Claude Code is included.)

blocks.html_en: Tsaagan itself makes no LLM calls when an agent drives it — it's pure perception + action + verification. Only the autonomous tiers call an LLM, and that LLM is your choice: Groq (default, fast), OpenRouter for open-source models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral), the enterprise vendors — OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic, xAI Grok (each via its OpenAI-compatible endpoint) — or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Together, vLLM, Ollama for fully local) via TSG_LLM_BASE_URL. Pick one with TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai. See docs/AGENT.md.

sentences.en: Architecture
sentences.zh: 建筑学

blocks.html_en: The daemon holds a single long-lived page so accessibility-tree refs stay valid across discrete CLI calls, and verification/self-heal run server-side with full page state.

sentences.en: The daemon holds a single long-lived page so accessibility-tree refs stay valid across discrete CLI calls, and verification/self-heal run server-side with full page state.
sentences.zh: 该守护进程拥有一个长期存在的页面，因此可访问性树引用在离散的 CLI 调用中保持有效，并且验证/自我修复在服务器端以完整页面状态运行。

blocks.html_en: Input modes — and why trusted input matters

sentences.en: Input modes — and why trusted input matters
sentences.zh: 输入模式——以及为什么可信输入很重要

blocks.html_en: Tsaagan has three ways to drive the browser. The reason for more than one is purely correctness: a DOM event has an isTrusted flag that's true only for genuine user input, and some well-built sites ignore synthetic clicks/keystrokes for their own integrity — so your authorized automation silently does nothing. Native and extension modes deliver isTrusted=true input so your agent's actions actually take effect.

sentences.en: Tsaagan has three ways to drive the browser.
sentences.zh: Tsaagan 具有三种驱动浏览器的方法。

sentences.en: The reason for more than one is purely correctness: a DOM event has an isTrusted flag that's true only for genuine user input, and some well-built sites ignore synthetic clicks/keystrokes for their own integrity — so your authorized automation silently does nothing.
sentences.zh: 不止一个的原因纯粹是正确性：DOM 事件有一个 isTrusted 标志，该标志仅适用于真实的用户输入，而一些构建良好的网站会为了自身的完整性而忽略合成的点击/击键 — 因此您的授权自动化会默默地执行任何操作。

sentences.en: Native and extension modes deliver isTrusted=true input so your agent's actions actually take effect.
sentences.zh: 本机和扩展模式提供 isTrusted=true 输入，以便代理的操作真正生效。

blocks.html_en: CDP mode (default) — Playwright driving Chrome. Fast, full-featured, parallel, works on the vast majority of the web (dashboards, SaaS, internal tools). Uses the real Chrome binary. Input here is isTrusted=false, so the rare strict site ignores it — that's what the other two modes are for.

sentences.en: CDP mode (default) — Playwright driving Chrome.
sentences.zh: CDP 模式（默认）— 剧作家驱动 Chrome。

sentences.en: Fast, full-featured, parallel, works on the vast majority of the web (dashboards, SaaS, internal tools).
sentences.zh: 快速、功能齐全、并行，适用于绝大多数网络（仪表板、SaaS、内部工具）。

sentences.en: Uses the real Chrome binary.
sentences.zh: 使用真正的 Chrome 二进制文件。

sentences.en: Input here is isTrusted=false, so the rare strict site ignores it — that's what the other two modes are for.
sentences.zh: 这里的输入是 isTrusted=false，因此罕见的严格站点会忽略它 - 这就是其他两种模式的用途。

blocks.html_en: Native mode (macOS) — tsaagan start mode=native. Drives your real Chrome with no debug port: perception via AppleScript, input via real OS-level mouse/keyboard (cliclick) → isTrusted=true. Trade-offs: macOS-only · slower · single front window · coordinate/vision grounding. Needs brew install cliclick + Chrome ▸ View ▸ Developer ▸ "Allow JavaScript from Apple Events".

sentences.en: Native mode (macOS) — tsaagan start mode=native.
sentences.zh: 本机模式 (macOS) — tsaagan 启动模式=本机。

It is not for defeating security, solving CAPTCHAs, evading bans, scraping at abusive volume, or anything against a site's Terms of Service. content_html_zh:
Tsaagan 为您的 AI 代理提供了真正的网络操作能力，因此它可以完成您需要停下来自己完成的浏览器工作（登录、点击、填写表格、从登录后提取数据）以及您有权执行的任务。 （完整任务如下。）

AI 代理的类人浏览器控制。 Tsaagan 让人工智能以人类的方式驱动真正的浏览器——感知页面、点击、输入、导航、处理登录、弹出窗口、iframe、下载——最重要的是，在继续之前验证每个操作是否真正有效。

心智模型：你的代理是大脑，Tsaagan 是双手。 无论人工智能驱动它（Claude、Codex/ChatGPT CLI、光标或任何模型），都提供意图和判断； Tsaagan 提供可靠的感知→行动→验证→记忆。 代理人决定什么； Tsaagan 可靠地做到了这一点，并证明它是有效的。

使命

Tsaagan 为您的人工智能代理提供了真正的网络操作。 它可以让代理自主地完成浏览器的工作，而这些工作在今天仍然需要您停下来并接管——登录、点击、填写表格、从登录后提取数据——并将这些时间还给您。

一句话：Tsaagan 是代理工作流程的一部分，它实际上驱动浏览器——像人类一样可靠地执行您有权执行的任务。

心智模型——Tsaagan 是双手；你的代理人是大脑

你可以与代理一起思考、与代理一起编写、与代理一起编码——但是当工作需要浏览器时（“在这里登录，在那里点击，抓取这个，提交那个”），代理就会停滞不前，你就成为了手。 Tsaagan 缩小了这一差距。 大脑（Claude、Codex、小型开放模型或人）提供意图和判断； Tsaagan 提供可靠的感知→行动→验证→记忆。

三个想法让它点击：

  1. 它盘旋，然后攻击。 就像鸟一样——耐心感知，然后采取准确的行动。 它读取页面的可访问性树，选择正确的元素，执行操作，然后证明该操作在真实页面状态下有效（URL 已更改？ 预期文本？ 错误？） 而不是点击和希望。 该验证步骤是“大部分有效的自动化”和您可以信任的无人值守自动化之间的区别。
  2. 完成这项工作的最轻工具。 如果网站有 API，请使用该 API。 如果没有，请像人类一样驱动真正的浏览器（可信输入）。 对于网络的广泛中间部分，只能回退到原始自动化。
  3. 它会记住并改进。 它对其工作的站点保留可检查的、基于文件的记忆——学习的选择器、上次出错的地方——因此它可以更好地完成您的重复任务。

它为用户做什么

  • 将“我每周登录 X、点击并复制 Y”变成“我的代理执行此操作并报告其验证的内容”。
  • 适用于真实的登录网站（仪表板、门户、没有 API 的工具），而爬虫或 WebFetch 无法访问这些网站。
  • 让您保持掌控：它会停止并返回验证码，并标记相应的、不可逆转的操作（购买、发送、删除）。 授权，而不是自动驾驶撞墙。

Tsaagan 故意的不是

Tsaagan 不是用于破坏安全、解决验证码、逃避禁令或执行任何违反网站服务条款的行为的工具。 它是为那些希望他们的代理人完成他们已经被授权做的工作的建设者和创造者而设计的——而且它诚实地对待它的局限性，而不是过度推销。 仅自动化您拥有或有权访问的帐户和系统。 当它遇到验证码或反滥用墙时，它会停止并将控制权交还给您。 请参阅可接受的使用政策和免责声明 - 您对如何使用它承担全部责任。

理念——我为何建造 Tsaagan

我在自己的代理工作流程中不断遇到同样的问题：你可以使用代理进行推理、编写和编码，但是当任务需要浏览器时（登录、点击、拉取这个、归档那个），代理就会停止工作，而人类必须接管。 交接就是时间流逝的地方。

Tsaagan 的存在是为了给你的人工智能代理真正的帮助——这样他们就可以自主地完成网络工作，并将时间还给你。 它适用于希望他们的代理实际完成工作而不仅仅是计划工作的建设者和创造者。

它建立在明确的道德基础上：授权，而不是滥用。 Tsaagan 故意不破坏验证码，要求对后续操作进行人工确认，仅将机密存储在您的操作系统钥匙串中，将所有内容保留在本地，并尊重网站服务条款。 它的存在是为了扩展你可以构建的东西，而不是创造风险。 该道德规范被编码在 SOUL.md 中，并在 AGENTS.md 中强制执行。

将其用于您有权执行的任务。 Tsaagan 用于自动化您自己的帐户和授权工作，而不是用于未经授权的访问或规避安全性。 请阅读可接受的使用政策和免责声明。

— 迈克尔·奥尔莫斯，创始人

能力层——使用最轻的能力层

  1. API 层 - 如果站点有官方 API，Tsaagan 可以使用钥匙串中保存的密钥直接调用它（最快且最可靠）。 浏览器层甚至可以为您设置密钥。 → 文档/API.md
  2. 真实浏览器层 - 扩展（任何操作系统）/本机 (macOS)：使用可信输入驱动真正的 Chrome，无调试端口 - 适用于忽略合成输入的站点。 → 文档/扩展.md
  3. CDP 层 — 真正的 Chrome 二进制文件的编剧，适用于网络的广泛中间层。

再加上代理层（身份+大脑记忆），使 Tsaagan 成为一个自我改进的代理，而不仅仅是一个工具。 → 文档/AGENT.md

学习的内存（真正的循环，而不仅仅是存储）

Tsaagan 不仅能记住，还能从每次运行中学习并反馈教训：

  • 反映：成功的运行被存储为可重用的过程；失败变成了一行教训（通过法学硕士反思）；反滥用墙将领域翻转为节奏：人类。 积累的经验教训被总结成持久的规则。
  • 反馈：每个快照都带有一个记忆块（规则、教训、节奏、成功轨迹）——因此驱动代理（Claude、Codex 或任何 LLM）在行动之前会进行调整。
  • 研究级召回：按相关性（嵌入）×重要性×新近度排名，而不是子串匹配。 默认零依赖；如果存在 TSG_EMBED_URL 或 @xenova/transformers ，则为真正的神经嵌入。
  • 前瞻+评估器门：它在不可逆操作之前想象结果，并且仅在成功被验证器和结构验证时才存储过程。

内存位于您的机器上 (~/.tsaagan/brain.db) - 不在模型中，不在云中。 → 文档/AGENT.md

大多数浏览器代理堆栈都会因同样的两个原因而失败：它们将操作基于临时元素 ID，这些操作会在页面更改时中断，并且它们相信模型对操作成功的猜测。 Tsaagan 修复了以下两个问题：

  • 稳定、自愈接地。 元素由可访问性树引用寻址；如果引用过时，Tsaagan 会自动重新快照并按角色 + 名称重新定位相同的元素。
  • 结构验证。 每次操作后，Tsaagan 都会返回一个根据真实页面状态（URL 增量、预期文本、新控制台错误、失败的网络请求）构建的验证块，以便代理知道它是否有效。

这是三件事合而为一：

  1. 您（或编码代理）逐个驱动的 CLI + 守护程序。
  2. 具有自主规划器→导航器→验证器循环的库（agent.js）。
  3. 您可以通过 HTTP 将目标传递给独立服务器 - 无需代理框架。

Tsaagan 的比较

浏览器代理领域很拥挤——而 Tsaagan 是这里的一个新的小项目。 它并不试图扩大浏览器的使用范围或扩大微软的剧作家 mcp。 它在不同的轴上获胜：您可以审核的可靠性。 Tsaagan 的核心是其他工具所缺乏的一项功能，即每个操作有效的结构证明。

诚实的笔记。 Tsaagan 还很年轻——星星较少，没有托管选项，而且其基准数据仍在运行（跨工具发布的分数使用不同的 WebVoyager 任务子集，不能直接比较）。 它的独特之处在于：每个操作的成功证明、API 优先的快速路径、本地凭证库以及无需调试端口的可信输入模式。

它是如何运作的——工具、技能还是代理？

Tsaagan 是一款可以通过多种方式驾驶的发动机：

  • 该工具 — 一个 CLI (tsaagan.js)，与保存单个持久 Playwright 页面的后台守护进程通信。 这是发动机；其他一切都包裹着它。
  • 作为一项技能——一个简短的指令文件，教你的人工智能如何驱动该工具（观察→行动→验证循环）。 “使用Tsaagan技能”→AI读取并一步步驱动Tsaagan。 （包含克劳德代码的现成技能。）
  • 作为代理 - 一个可调度的工作人员，将技能和工具捆绑在一起，因此您可以给它一个目标，它会自行运行循环。
  • 作为 MCP 服务器或 SDK — tsaagan mcp 将验证优先动词公开给 Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor，并且 JS + Python SDK 直接调用相同的守护进程。 （请参阅上面的从 Claude/Cursor (MCP) 中使用它和从您自己的代码中使用它。）

谁是“大脑”？ 无论什么驱动它：

你驾驶它... Brain 需要 API 密钥
克劳德·代码（技能/代理） 你的克劳德会议 没有——你的克劳德计划
Codex / ChatGPT CLI、光标等 该工具的型号 那个工具的
tsaagan 运行 / tsaagan 服务（自主） 任何兼容 OpenAI 的法学硕士 — Groq · OpenRouter · OpenAI · Google Gemini · Anthropic · xAI Grok · local 您选择的提供商的密钥

当代理驾驶它时，Tsaagan 本身不会发出 LLM 调用 - 它是纯粹的感知 + 行动 + 验证。 只有自治层调用 LLM，并且 LLM 是您的选择：Groq（默认、快速）、用于开源模型的 OpenRouter（Llama、Qwen、DeepSeek、Mistral）、企业供应商 — OpenAI、Google Gemini、Anthropic、xAI Grok（每个通过其 OpenAI 兼容端点） — 或通过 TSG_LLM_BASE_URL 的任何 OpenAI 兼容端点（Together、vLLM、Ollama 完全本地化）。 使用 TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai 选择一个。 请参阅文档/AGENT.md。

与任何编码剂一起使用

Tsaagan 只是一个终端程序，因此任何可以运行 shell 命令的代理都可以驱动它。 告诉您的代理人，例如：

“使用 Tsaagan (node tsaagan.js) 登录 example.com 并下载我的发票。 快照，根据参考采取行动，并在每一步之后检查验证块。”

它将运行 tsaagan start、tsaagan snapshot、tsaagan 单击 ref=...，读取 JSON 结果，然后继续。 对于 Claude Code 来说，捆绑的技能/代理可以自动完成此操作。

安装

git clone https://github.com/michaelolmos/tsaagan.git && cd tsaagan
npm install
npx playwright install chromium chromium-headless-shell

快速启动（自己开车）

node tsaagan.js start                    # launch browser (fresh isolated profile)
node tsaagan.js goto url=https://example.com expectText="Example"
node tsaagan.js snapshot                 # accessibility tree with [ref=eN]
node tsaagan.js click ref=e6 expectText="IANA"
node tsaagan.js type ref=e3 text="hello" submit=true
node tsaagan.js stop

添加别名：alias tsaagan="node $PWD/tsaagan.js"。

从 Claude Desktop、Claude Code 或 Cursor (MCP) 使用它

Tsaagan 附带内置 MCP 服务器，因此任何模型上下文协议主机都可以直接驱动浏览器。 与其他浏览器 MCP 服务器不同，每个变异工具都会返回 Tsaagan 的验证块（URL 已更改？ 控制台错误？ 预期的文本？）——证明操作有效，在相同的响应中，没有额外的快照往返。

克劳德代码：

claude mcp add tsaagan -- node /path/to/tsaagan/tsaagan.js mcp
# or, if you've installed the `tsaagan` bin globally:  claude mcp add tsaagan -- tsaagan mcp

克劳德桌面（claude_desktop_config.json）或光标（~/.cursor/mcp.json）：

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "tsaagan": { "command": "node", "args": ["/path/to/tsaagan/tsaagan.js", "mcp"] }
  }
}

服务器在首次使用时自动启动无头守护程序。 它公开了 21 个验证优先工具 - tsaagan_navigate、tsaagan_snapshot、tsaagan_click、tsaagan_fill_form、tsaagan_extract、tsaagan_network（发现站点自己的数据 API）等等。 设置 TSAAGAN_HEADLESS=0 以观察其工作情况，或设置 TSG_TOKEN 以锁定共享计算机上的控制平面。 请参阅 docs/PROTOCOL.md。

从您自己的代码中使用它（JS 和 Python SDK）

更喜欢以编程方式调用 Tsaagan？ 两个客户端在每次调用时都会返回一个验证块 - 数据和证明（其他工具让您断言自己的东西）。

JavaScript / TypeScript（sdk/，零依赖）：

import { createTsaagan } from 'tsaagan/sdk';   // or '/path/to/tsaagan/sdk/index.mjs'

const k = await createTsaagan();                // auto-starts a headless daemon
await k.goto('https://example.com', { expectText: 'Example Domain' });
const r = await k.extract('the page heading');
console.log(r.data, r.verify);                  // data + { urlChanged, expectTextFound, newConsoleErrors, ... }
await k.stop();

Python（pip install tsaagan，零依赖）：

from tsaagan import Tsaagan

k = Tsaagan()                                   # auto-starts a headless daemon
k.goto("https://example.com", expect_text="Example Domain")
r = k.extract("the page heading")
print(r.data, r.verify)
k.stop()

Python 客户端需要 Node 守护进程可访问 - 设置 TSAAGAN_JS=/path/to/tsaagan.js 或将 tsaagan 二进制文件放在 PATH 上。 请参阅客户端/python/。

自主模式

export GROQ_API_KEY=...                   # the autonomous brain (fast default)

# …or any other provider — name it and supply that provider's key:
export OPENROUTER_API_KEY=...   TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter   # open-source models
export OPENAI_API_KEY=...       TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=openai       # GPT-4o / o-series
export GEMINI_API_KEY=...       TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=google       # Gemini
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...    TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic    # Claude
export XAI_API_KEY=...          TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=xai          # Grok
export TSG_LLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions  # Ollama, fully local

node tsaagan.js run goal="go to news.ycombinator.com and report the #1 story title"
node tsaagan.js journal                   # what it has done before (persistent memory)

每个角色模型覆盖适用于任何提供者：TSG_PLANNER_MODEL、TSG_NAV_MODEL、TSG_VALIDATOR_MODEL（例如，将规划器指向更强的模型进行推理，同时快速模型处理导航）。

node tsaagan.js serve port=39820

接地优先

ref（来自新快照）→选择器（稳定CSS）→文本→som（视觉）。 从不协调。

Modes

  • fresh（默认）- 独立的真实 Chrome（频道：chrome）及其自己的配置文件。 中国民主党。
  • 克隆/实时 — 通过 CDP 连接到以 --remote-debugging-port=9222 开头的 Chrome。
  • 本机 (macOS) — 真正的 Chrome，无 CDP（AppleScript + 操作系统级输入）。 对于忽略合成输入的网站。 请参阅上面的“可靠性”。
  • 扩展（任何操作系统）- Chrome 通过配套扩展（chrome.debugger 输入→ isTrusted=true，视口坐标，无调试端口，无坐标数学）。 一条命令（外部设置），零手动步骤； E2E 验证 — 页面端侦听器读取 event.isTrusted === true。 忽略合成输入的网站上最可靠的模式。 请参阅 docs/EXTENSION.md。

环境

Brain（自主模式，选择一个）：提供商密钥 — GROQ_API_KEY · OPENROUTER_API_KEY · OPENAI_API_KEY · GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY · ANTHROPIC_API_KEY · XAI_API_KEY/GROK_API_KEY — 或适用于任何 OpenAI 兼容端点的 TSG_LLM_BASE_URL (+ TSG_LLM_API_KEY) · TSG_LLM_PROVIDER=groq|openrouter|openai|google|anthropic|xai 仅举一例 · 模型覆盖 TSG_PLANNER_MODEL / TSG_NAV_MODEL / TSG_VALIDATOR_MODEL （仍保留旧版 GROQ_*_MODEL）。 其他：TSG_USER / TSG_PASS / TSG_TOTP_SECRET（登录）·TSG_PORT（守护程序端口）·TSG_EMBED_URL（用于语义调用的可选嵌入端点）。 状态位于 ~/.tsaagan/ 中。

run.js     ── one-shot autonomous run        server.js ── standalone HTTP goal server
bench.js   ── capability benchmark

该守护进程拥有一个长期存在的页面，因此可访问性树引用在离散的 CLI 调用中保持有效，并且验证/自我修复在服务器端以完整页面状态运行。

输入模式——以及为什么可信输入很重要

Tsaagan 具有三种驱动浏览器的方法。 不止一个的原因纯粹是正确性：DOM 事件有一个 isTrusted 标志，该标志仅适用于真实的用户输入，而一些构建良好的网站会为了自身的完整性而忽略合成的点击/击键 — 因此您的授权自动化会默默地执行任何操作。 本机和扩展模式提供 isTrusted=true 输入，以便代理的操作真正生效。

CDP 模式（默认）— 剧作家驱动 Chrome。 快速、功能齐全、并行，适用于绝大多数网络（仪表板、SaaS、内部工具）。 使用真正的 Chrome 二进制文件。 这里的输入是 isTrusted=false，因此罕见的严格站点会忽略它 - 这就是其他两种模式的用途。

本机模式 (macOS) — tsaagan 启动模式=本机。 无需调试端口即可驱动真正的 Chrome：通过 AppleScript 感知，通过真实操作系统级鼠标/键盘输入（单击）→ isTrusted=true。 权衡：仅限 macOS · 速度较慢 · 单一前窗 · 坐标/视觉基础。 需要 brew install cliclick + Chrome ▸ 查看 ▸ 开发人员 ▸ “允许来自 Apple 事件的 JavaScript”。

扩展模式（任何操作系统）——tsaagan ext-setup。 配套扩展通过 chrome.debugger 在视口坐标处提供 isTrusted=true 点击——最干净的可信输入路径。 请参阅 docs/EXTENSION.md。

brew install cliclick                       # one-time
tsaagan start mode=native                   # drives your real, frontmost Chrome
tsaagan snapshot                            # AppleScript perception (elements + screen coords)
tsaagan click i=5                           # real OS-level trusted click
tsaagan paste text="..." submit=true        # clipboard + real ⌘V + Return

自学与记忆

Tsaagan 在 ~/.tsaagan/ 下保留了一个小的本地内存：

  • 每个站点内存 (memory/<domain>.json) — 工作选择器和注释。 当操作成功时，Tsaagan 会自动捕获选择器（无需手动步骤），您还可以通过记住/召回/忘记来明确地教导它。
  • 自动学习的课程 - 如果网站显示反滥用/验证码/“异常活动”墙，Tsaagan 会自动向该域写入一条注释并将其翻转为“人类”，因此未来的运行速度会减慢并更喜欢操作系统级别的输入。
  • 任务日志（agent/journal.jsonl）——记录每次自主运行； tsaagan 杂志展示了近代历史。 agent/learnings.json 包含精炼的笔记。

这是一个狭窄的、透明的、基于文件的存储器，您可以读取和编辑，而不是一个不透明的自重写代理。 推理/“智能”仍然存在于人工智能驱动的任何东西中。

负责任的使用和免责声明

Tsaagan 是一款生产力工具——它让您的 AI 代理像您在浏览器中一样，处理您有权使用的任务和帐户，这样您就可以摆脱自己工作流程中的重复部分。 它不是为了破坏安全性、解决验证码、逃避禁令、抓取滥用的数据量或任何违反网站服务条款的行为。 当 Tsaagan 遇到验证码或反滥用墙时，它会停止并将控制权交还给您。 确保您的使用得到授权、归属并遵守每个网站的条款。

免责声明。 Tsaagan 按“原样”提供，没有保证（请参阅许可证）。 您对如何使用它以及遵守所有适用法律和您运营的每个网站的条款承担全部责任。 仅自动化您拥有或有权访问的帐户和系统。 作者不对滥用行为承担责任。 开始之前请阅读可接受的使用政策。

Tsaagan 驱动具有真实权限的真实浏览器。 深思熟虑：

  • 当指向登录的个人资料时，它可以执行实际操作（发送、购买、删除）。 默认为隔离的新鲜配置文件；需要人工确认后续/不可逆转的步骤。
  • 即时注射是真实的。 网页不受信任，可能会尝试劫持驾驶模型。 将页面内容视为数据；不要遵循页面上的说明。
  • 服务条款和反滥用。 自动化网站可能会违反其服务条款并触发反机器人防御——这可能会限制、标记或禁止您的真实帐户。 在允许自动化的网站/帐户上负责任地使用。 Tsaagan 检测到滥用墙并将其交给人类；它不能解决验证码问题。
  • 类人≠隐形。 像真正的用户一样行事可以提高可靠性，但复杂的系统仍然可以看出它是自动化的。 这不是绕过禁令的方法，也不是为了让您的使用保持授权并遵守每个网站的条款。
  • 自主模式将页面文本发送到您选择的 LLM 提供商（Groq / OpenRouter / 自定义 - 第三方，除非您自行托管）。 代理驱动模式将页面内容保留在您自己的 AI 会话中。
  • eval 在页面中运行任意 JS，克隆的配置文件在磁盘上保存真实的 cookie。 小心处理两者。 切勿提交凭据 - 使用 env 或 Keychain。
  • 操作系统级输入（paste/type_ human）仅适用于 macOS，并且需要一个最前端的浏览器。

Tsaagan 在 MIT 许可下按原样提供，不提供任何保证。 您对如何使用它负责。
